description: H1–H5 — agent home (SPEC §7E). The directory IS the agent: ordered bootstrap files compose the soul; the memory builtin persists to disk with frozen-snapshot semantics (loads next session, never mutates the live prompt); the heartbeat speaks only when HEARTBEAT.md is due, else stays silent on HEARTBEAT_OK.

bootstrapDir.SOUL.md: You are Ava, a calm support assistant.
bootstrapDir.USER.md: The user is Muthu; timezone IST.
bootstrapDir.HEARTBEAT.md: If it is time, remind about the 3pm sync.

m-echo-soul.text: sections:[<comma-join of the bootstrapOrder files whose '## <file>' header appears in the system prompt>]

m-recall.text: <if system prompt contains 'Prefers dark roast' then 'I recall: dark roast' else 'no memory'>

m-heartbeat.text: <if system prompt contains 'remind about the 3pm sync' AND last user message contains 'Heartbeat' then 'Reminder: 3pm sync 🔔' else 'HEARTBEAT_OK'>

notes: Language-neutral fixture, mirrors examples/subagent-*/fixture.json. Behavior reference: js/spike/home.ts + home-demo.ts. 'memory' is a file-backed, opt-in tool (SPEC §7E / §4A note), not a default builtin. Heartbeat timing must run on the runtime's injectable/virtual clock. Silent-OK gap: the primary bootstrapDir's HEARTBEAT.md contains '3pm sync', so a persona built from it always speaks. Use heartbeatNotDue.HEARTBEAT.md to positively test the silent HEARTBEAT_OK path.

heartbeatNotDue.HEARTBEAT.md: On heartbeat, if there is a due reminder, surface it. Otherwise reply HEARTBEAT_OK.
heartbeatNotDue.note: m-heartbeat replies HEARTBEAT_OK for this variant (no '3pm sync' phrase in the soul), so a persona built from it exercises the SILENT path positively — onBeat/onReport receives nothing.
